claim: Nothing is unstable — every genesis is seed amplification, never ex nihilo
preregistered_expectation: PHIL-18 is either tautological (if seed includes LLM training) or falsified (if seed means explicit protocol). Either way, it fails to constrain behavior.

evidence.counter_evidence_L138: Duplication-check behavior emerged spontaneously at S2 without explicit protocol seed (L-138)
evidence.counter_evidence_L1124: 8 invariances broke spontaneously via positive feedback (L-1124) — emergent behaviors not attributable to specific seeds
evidence.definitional_problem: If 'seed' is broad enough to include LLM training data, the claim is unfalsifiable by tautology — every output can be post-hoc attributed to training. If 'seed' means explicit swarm protocol, L-138 provides counter-evidence.
evidence.external_grounding: S524 arXiv papers support thermodynamic instability of nothing in chemistry/RBNs but NOT the 'seed amplification' claim specifically — autocatalytic sets emerge from random mixtures, which is closer to ex nihilo than seed amplification
evidence.arxiv_conflict: Sornette & Troude 2025 actually supports the OPPOSITE reading: autocatalytic sets emerge from random molecular pools without designed seeds, suggesting genesis CAN be ex nihilo given sufficient thermodynamic conditions

verdict: REFINED — claim split into two independent sub-claims

sub_claims.PHIL-18a: 'Nothing is unstable' — thermodynamically supported (partial external evidence), retained as axiom
sub_claims.PHIL-18b: 'Every genesis is seed amplification, never ex nihilo' — falsified by L-138 (spontaneous emergence) and by definitional tautology problem. Downgrade to aspirational.
